verses.text: 'God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble,' so His people will not fear though the earth itself be moved. He is near and sufficient. When the world shakes, the believer's anchor holds in God.

'God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble,' so His people will not fear though the earth itself be moved. He is near and sufficient. When the world shakes, the believer's anchor holds in God.

'God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble,' so His people will not fear though the earth itself be moved. He is near and sufficient. When the world shakes, the believer's anchor holds in God.
A river gladdens the city of God, for 'God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved.' His presence is her security. The Church stands firm because her God dwells within her.
A river gladdens the city of God, for 'God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved.' His presence is her security. The Church stands firm because her God dwells within her.

The nations raged and kingdoms tottered, but God 'uttered his voice, the earth melted.' 'The LORD of hosts is with us.' A word from Him stills the uproar. The God of armies is the refuge of His people.

The nations raged and kingdoms tottered, but God 'uttered his voice, the earth melted.' 'The LORD of hosts is with us.' A word from Him stills the uproar. The God of armies is the refuge of His people.
'Come, behold the works of the LORD,' who ends wars, breaks the bow, and burns the chariot. He is the great peacemaker. The God who scatters armies will bring lasting peace at last.
'Come, behold the works of the LORD,' who ends wars, breaks the bow, and burns the chariot. He is the great peacemaker. The God who scatters armies will bring lasting peace at last.
'Be still, and know that I am God,' He says; He will be exalted among the nations. The command silences our striving. To cease our frantic fear and trust His sovereign reign is the soul's true rest.
'Be still, and know that I am God,' He says; He will be exalted among the nations. The command silences our striving. To cease our frantic fear and trust His sovereign reign is the soul's true rest.
